headline: Best adventure picks to stream when you want something better than another lazy scroll
meta_description: Need better adventure picks for tonight? Here are the titles actually worth starting first, without the usual generic streaming-list fluff.
subtitle: Featuring The Monkey King, True Spirit, and The Sea Beast — plus more adventure picks that deserve real attention.

hero_image.title: Best adventure picks to stream when you want something better than another lazy scroll
hero_image.subtitle: Featuring The Monkey King, True Spirit, and The Sea Beast — plus more adventure picks that deserve real attention.

selected_titles.title: The Monkey King
selected_titles.description: An animated adventure based on the legendary Monkey King from Chinese folklore.

selected_titles.title: True Spirit
selected_titles.description: A drama about a young girl setting out to become the youngest person to sail solo around the world.

selected_titles.title: The Sea Beast
selected_titles.description: An animated adventure about a legendary sea monster hunter who finds an unlikely friend.

selected_titles.title: do little
selected_titles.description: A veterinarian who talks to animals embarks on a whimsical journey.

selected_titles.title: Hilda
selected_titles.description: An animated series about a fearless girl exploring a magical world full of creatures.

selected_titles.title: Jungle
selected_titles.description: A man survives the Amazon rainforest alone.

selected_titles.title: Mogawli
selected_titles.description: An adventure film about a boy raised by wolves in the jungle, based on 'The Jungle Book'.

cta.headline: Want a custom watchlist instead of generic streaming lists?
cta.body: Message MovieJam on WhatsApp and get 3 tailored picks based on your mood, genre, and how much brainpower you want to spend tonight.

internal_link_defaults.title: How to get custom movie recs on WhatsApp
Why endless scrolling ruins movie night

style_rules: Lead with a viral, opinionated hook in the first paragraph.
Keep the same vibe as the existing MyMovieJam blog: sharp, audience-first, clean, punchy, and honest.
Write like a real viewer, not a neutral encyclopedia.
Do not fake plot details, awards, or availability.
If the CSV data is thin, make the opinion narrower instead of inventing facts.
Use the MyMovieJam visual theme and CTA style already present on the site.
SEO first, but never at the cost of sounding robotic.
Use clear subheads, quick picks, FAQs, and a strong WhatsApp CTA.
notes_for_writer: Tell the truth if the list mixes films and series.
Write as a sharp viewer with taste, not a neutral database.
Pick clear winners, easy starters, and mood-based recommendations.
Use a clear WhatsApp CTA at the end.
